title: Glazed Jamaican Black Beans Tagine
description: Jammy, chewy, festive Jamaican tagine of black beans glazed with sweet maple syrup and coconut milk, finished with a dollop of sour cream.

ingredients.name: Cooked black beans
ingredients.notes: cooked from dried beans or canned, drained

instructions: Heat the infused olive oil in the ceramic tagine over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, ginger, and scotch bonnet pepper, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Sprinkle in the thyme, cumin, and soy sauce, mixing well and letting the spices toast for 30 seconds.
Add the cooked black beans, light coconut milk, and maple syrup, stirring to combine everything.
Bring the mixture to a gentle simmer, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and let it cook for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Check the seasoning, and if desired, add more soy sauce or maple syrup for depth.
Remove from heat, stir in a squeeze of lime juice, then transfer to a serving dish and top with a dollop of sour cream before serving.
